query:
INSERT INTO blog_posts (
  id,
  title,
  slug,
  excerpt,
  content,
  authorName,
  tags,
  featuredImageUrl,
  publishedAt,
  createdAt,
  updatedAt
) VALUES (
  'blog-china-trip-planning-guide',
  'Planning Your First China Trip: The Itinerary Questions Everyone Asks (Keep Updating)',
  'planning-first-china-trip-itinerary-questions',
  'We have helped over 40 first-time visitors plan their China trips. Here are honest answers to the questions that keep coming up, from itinerary length to weather to traveling with kids.',
  'We have helped over 40 first-time visitors plan their China trips across Facebook and Reddit travel groups. The same questions keep coming up. Here are the honest answers, drawn from real conversations with real travelers. We will keep updating this post as new common themes emerge. Beijing deserves 4 to 5 nights minimum. Shanghai is more manageable in size but rewards slowing down with 3 to 4 nights. Xi an needs 3 nights because the Terracotta Warriors are a full day experience. Chengdu deserves 3 nights for pandas, Sichuan food, and Leshan Giant Buddha. Chongqing is dramatic and worth 2 to 3 nights. Zhangjiajie needs 3 nights. The travelers who enjoy China most are the ones who do fewer cities better, not more cities faster. The Golden Triangle (Beijing, Shanghai, Xi an) comes up constantly because it works. Three iconic cities, logical routing, well-connected by high speed train. For 10 to 14 days it is hard to beat. If you have under 10 days, the Golden Triangle gets squeezed. If you have 3 weeks or more, do the Golden Triangle and add a nature destination like Zhangjiajie, or a slower city like Chengdu or Chongqing. High speed trains are almost always the right call over domestic flights once you factor in airport time. Beijing to Xi an overnight sleeper is a classic. Xi an to Shanghai in 6 hours on a day train is comfortable and scenic. Keep Xi an, especially for a first trip. The Terracotta Warriors are genuinely one of the most impressive things you will see anywhere in the world. The Muslim Quarter has some of the best street food in China. Guangzhou is a great city but it skews modern and everyday. Save it for a second trip when you want to go deeper rather than broader. China has several major holidays where domestic travel spikes significantly. Chinese New Year is the biggest migration event in the world. Trains and flights book out weeks in advance. Labour Day Golden Week is a significant travel peak. National Day Golden Week is the busiest travel period after Chinese New Year. If your trip overlaps with any of these, spend the holiday days in a large city rather than a scenic mountain area. June through August is hot and humid across most of eastern China. Shanghai, Beijing, and Chongqing can all exceed 35 degrees. Zhangjiajie in spring and early summer is often misty and rainy. October is widely considered the best month for China travel. Shanghai and Beijing are the most stroller-friendly cities. Chongqing is the trickiest because the city is extremely hilly and vertical. For food, China is actually great for young children. Plain steamed rice, congee, steamed egg, noodles, and dumplings are mild, soft, and available everywhere. Build in at least one full free day per week and do not schedule it. The travelers who come back most satisfied are rarely the ones who ticked off the most cities. Fuxing Park on a weekend morning offers ballroom dancing, tai chi, card games, and zero tourists. Wukang Road rewards lingering. Shengjianbao from a street spot, not a restaurant, will ruin the fancy version for you permanently. Suzhou or Zhujiajiao as a day trip offers classical gardens and canals. If you want to go deeper on what everyday Shanghai life actually looks like, we run +1 Chopsticks, a home dining experience with our local English-speaking family.',
  'Guest Contributor',
  '[\"travel-tips\", \"china-travel\", \"itinerary-planning\"]',
  'https://d2xsxph8kpxj0f.cloudfront.net/310519663228681359/mkW6ExSEHJcqGWsa6M4fqn/china-trip-planning-thumbnail-kt9nzp5aRKo7Jw7PusDowi.webp',
  NOW(),
  NOW(),
  NOW()
);
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway03.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user JF4SDJ8UQnAgkD3.root --database mkW6ExSEHJcqGWsa6M4fqn --execute INSERT INTO blog_posts (
  id,
  title,
  slug,
  excerpt,
  content,
  authorName,
  tags,
  featuredImageUrl,
  publishedAt,
  createdAt,
  updatedAt
) VALUES (
